title: Career Agency, Success Criteria, and Search Constraints · Hiring Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A hiring panel compares near-miss decisions for career agency, success criteria, and search constraints and accepts only role-relevant, truthful, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for learner-owned career decision charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date.
Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.
Model time, money, energy, care, health, and learning capacity under conservative search scenarios and escalation thresholds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Success Definition · changed case · Quiz Success Definition
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. The quiz variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Which action demonstrates Success Definition without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Success Definition · changed case · Quiz Success Definition.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date. For Success Definition · changed case · Quiz Success Definition, keep Success Definition connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. The quiz variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

The quiz variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

For Success Definition · changed case · Quiz Success Definition, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A successful search has one universal outcome: the highest available salary.

options.text: Define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for learner-owned career decision charter.
A successful search has one universal outcome: the highest available salary. That claim fails the success definition · changed case evidence boundary for Success Definition.

For success definition · changed case, apply Success Definition without its stated preconditions, omit the learner-owned career decision charter trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Success Definition for learner-owned career decision charter.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Agency Review Loop

The quiz variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Create a learner-controlled review cadence that adapts tactics from evidence without treating rejection as identity evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
